purpose: Synthetic kind=standard atoms for the M1c bloated arm.
rationale: Vault Persona/Research atoms use a different YAML schema (title/tags/date, no `kind:` field) and cannot be directly loaded by standards_pack.py. Rather than modify originals (forbidden) or contaminate vault content into a public PR, this fixture provides 30 plausible-but-fictional kind=standard atoms whose only job is to take up token budget in the bloated arm. The names are deliberately distinct from the `feedback_*` production prefix so they don't collide with real rules. The descriptions are written in the production tone (concise, imperative, technical) so they exercise the same attention pattern as real rules.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_implicit_coercion
atoms.description: Reject implicit type coercion at API boundaries; require explicit conversion with bounds validation.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_log_correlation_separation
atoms.description: Separate correlation-id namespace from trace-id namespace; never overload one for the other.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_idempotent_migrations
atoms.description: Schema migrations must be idempotent and reversible; assume retries and partial failures.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_quota_aware_retries
atoms.description: Retry loops must honor upstream quotas; exponential backoff with jitter, capped at the quota window.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_default_to_constant_time
atoms.description: Compare secrets and tokens with constant-time predicates; never short-circuit on length or first-byte mismatch.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_pin_dependency_majors
atoms.description: Pin major versions of third-party dependencies; floating majors invalidate prior security review.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_no_silent_truncation
atoms.description: Never silently truncate user input; reject with a clear error or paginate explicitly.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_separate_dry_run_from_apply
atoms.description: Destructive commands must support a dry-run flag that prints the plan without mutating state.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_observable_health_endpoints
atoms.description: Health checks must distinguish liveness from readiness; conflating them causes thundering-herd restarts.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_serialize_compound_writes
atoms.description: Compound writes that span multiple records require an explicit transaction or saga; never assume implicit ordering.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_default_paginate_lists
atoms.description: List endpoints default to a bounded page size; unbounded enumeration is opt-in with a paranoia cap.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_double_negation
atoms.description: Boolean flags and config names avoid double negation; prefer affirmative naming for clarity.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_normalize_timezones
atoms.description: Persist timestamps in UTC; convert at the presentation boundary, never in the storage layer.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_log_pii_redaction
atoms.description: Logs must redact emails, phone numbers, and tokens at the formatter, not at sink ingestion.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_test_failure_isolation
atoms.description: Tests must not share mutable global state; failure isolation requires per-test fixtures with cleanup.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_lazy_imports_in_loops
atoms.description: Module imports in hot paths or loops are a perf cliff; lift them to module scope when stable.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_explicit_charset
atoms.description: All text I/O specifies an explicit charset; relying on locale default is a portability footgun.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_separate_dev_and_prod_creds
atoms.description: Dev credentials must never authenticate against prod resources; key-rotation cadence is per environment.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_lock_then_check
atoms.description: Locks acquired for state mutation must re-check the precondition under the lock; race conditions hide behind optimism.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_log_decisions_not_data
atoms.description: Logs record decisions and outcomes, not raw inputs; auditors care about why, not what.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_god_objects
atoms.description: Single classes accumulating responsibilities are an obvious refactor signal; split by axis of change.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_external_calls_have_timeout
atoms.description: Every external call has a finite timeout; default infinite blocking is the root cause of cascading failure.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_paranoid_path_join
atoms.description: Path joining with user-controlled segments must resolve against a configured root and reject escapes.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_shadowing_builtins
atoms.description: Variable names that shadow language built-ins (list, dict, type, id) make stack traces confusing and code IDE-hostile.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_close_handles_explicitly
atoms.description: File handles, sockets, and DB connections close in `finally` or context managers; GC-driven cleanup is non-deterministic.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_async_propagation
atoms.description: Async functions must propagate context (request id, deadline, cancel signal) through the call chain explicitly.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_no_floating_decimals_for_money
atoms.description: Currency amounts use integer minor units or decimal libraries; floating-point arithmetic on money is a known bug class.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_event_schemas_versioned
atoms.description: Event payloads carry a schema version field from day one; backfill is harder than forward-compat.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_avoid_inheritance_for_reuse
atoms.description: Prefer composition over inheritance for code reuse; inheritance is for behavior contracts, not utility sharing.

atoms.name: synthetic_pad_dead_code_is_not_documentation
atoms.description: Commented-out code is not history; delete it and rely on version control for archaeology.
